things.name: Linlithgow Palace & Loch (UNESCO Tentative)
things.description: Magnificent ruined royal palace — birthplace of Mary Queen of Scots and James V — beside the beautiful Linlithgow Loch

things.name: Almondell & Calderwood Country Park
things.description: Beautiful riverside park in the Almond valley with woodland walks, a visitor centre and the historic Almondell Bridge

things.name: Livingston Designer Outlet
things.description: Scotland's largest outlet shopping centre with over 70 designer brands at discounted prices — a major retail destination

things.name: Cairnpapple Hill (Prehistoric Site)
things.description: Commanding hilltop site with one of Scotland's most significant Neolithic and Bronze Age ceremonial monuments, with views to both firths

things.name: Five Sisters Bings (Shale Heritage)
things.description: West Lothian's iconic shale oil waste bings — now a striking industrial heritage landscape and a site for walking and nature

things.name: Hopetoun House
things.description: Scotland's finest Adam mansion house on the Firth of Forth shore — magnificent state rooms, art collection and 100 acres of parkland

things.name: Beecraigs Country Park
things.description: Popular country park above Linlithgow with a loch for fishing and watersports, deer farm, archery range and camping

things.name: Edinburgh (Day Trip)
things.description: Scotland's capital just 30 minutes by train — Edinburgh Castle, Royal Mile, Holyrood Palace and the National Museum of Scotland

things.name: Almond Valley Heritage Centre
things.description: Award-winning family museum in a working farm — the story of West Lothian's shale oil industry, vintage farm machinery and animals

things.name: Bathgate Hills & Hillend Reservoir
things.description: Popular walking country above Livingston with Cairnpapple Hill, Knock Hill viewpoints and peaceful reservoir walks
